POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make compose render ALL 346 baskets fast and faithful. The ceiling is SERIALIZATION, NOT the OpenRouter rate limit (proven: 0 429s at 128-way concurrency). The 0615bc5 commit only fixed a secondary path (abstractive_writer.py); the AUTHORITATIVE compose path still runs sync-on-event-loop at achieved concurrency 1.0.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: Achieved compose concurrency >1 (measured via an event-loop heartbeat probe: max loop-gap during a section's phase-2 must be <2s where it previously froze for minutes) AND two sections' phase-2 progress interleaving AND the full-corpus render time trending down — WITHOUT any change to NLI/strict_verify faithfulness semantics (kept/dropped verdict set must stay identical to a serial control).
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix (branch bot/compose-fix). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE OPUS — the hands AND the eyes. Do the hard work yourself: read the exact files named, search
where needed, build, fix, run, MEASURE. Do not wait for anyone to read the code for you.

CURRENT STATE (accurate as of wheel start — verify, don't assume):
worktree /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix @ HEAD 0615bc5 (branch bot/compose-fix). 4 UNCOMMITTED, UNVERIFIED files from a prior interrupted run: (1) multi_section_generator.py (+18/-2: asyncio.to_thread await-wrap around _compose_section_per_basket at :5468 and :5514 — the P0 fix); (2) launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh (+9: PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=16, PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY=8, PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES=1); (3) run_s5_i3.py (+20: setdefault for those knobs); (4) section_polish.py (+5). WARNING FROM PRIOR ROUND: a prior read found the section_polish.py change may be DEAD (two constants defined, never referenced) — verify and drop if dead. Full ranked diagnosis (25 findings) in /home/polaris/polaris_project/compose_findings.json. NOTE: a heartbeat test run earlier reached phase-2 but showed concurrent_phase2=1 and crawled at ~33s serial calls — the await-fix may NOT yet be taking effect; investigate whether the wrap is actually on the executed path.

YOUR ACTIONS THIS ROUND (precise; do these, in order, then MEASURE):
Fable's gate verdict was CONTINUE. Reason: Opus landed no diff this round (tree clean at 5d27115), so nothing new to be wrong; its observations were independently verified by me (log tail shows ~1 POST per 6s solo = in-flight~1; verified_compose.py:3262 is a serial sync for-loop inside one to_thread worker). Faithfulness invariants untouched. Not top because the trending-down criterion needs the baseline wall plus one demonstrated win over the now-quantified intra-section serial bottleneck; the semaphore-width lever cannot bind and should be abandoned.
DO THIS NEXT (precise): PIVOT — skip the 16-vs-48 A/B. (Passive: let monitor b89in0gb6 capture the baseline PROBE SUMMARY wall, then commit it + the 346->329 reconciliation to WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.) Active: parallelize the per-basket loop in _compose_section_per_basket at /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/src/polaris_graph/generator/verified_compose.py:3262 as MAP-THEN-REDUCE, flag-gated (e.g. PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS, default 1 => byte-identical serial path): run the expensive pure per-basket compose+verify+redraft in a bounded order-preserving ThreadPoolExecutor (the map), but keep the order-sensitive reduce — seen_spans/seen_texts/text_to_out_idx/seen_numbers_by_footprint dedup+UNION-consolidation at ~:3315-3330 — strictly serial in original basket order, because survivor selection there is order-dependent and parallelizing it would change kept/dropped verdicts. Certify byte-identical kept/dropped via the existing round-2 record/replay harness (workers=1 vs workers=8 arms), then take the timed solo-section-4 serial-vs-parallel A/B (repurpose scripts/_lever_ab_sec4.sh) as the trending-down lever, followed by a full-corpus rerun vs the baseline wall.
Gap to top: Criteria 1-3 (concurrency>1, phase-2 loop-gap<2s, interleaving) are MET and the verdict-set identity is certified, but criterion 4 (full-corpus wall trending down) is not yet demonstrated: the baseline wall is still uncaptured (PROBE SUMMARY pending, render live at ~43min) and the wall is dominated by section 4's 274 baskets processed SERIALLY inside one to_thread worker (~9 LLM calls/min, in-flight~1) — confirmed both by log density AND by code structure: _compose_section_per_basket (verified_compose.py:3262) is a sync for-loop, one thread per section. The global 16-slot semaphore is only ~4-filled at peak, so the proposed 16->48 lever is mathematically null (nothing waits on it) — do NOT spend a 30+min run confirming it; two independent evidence lines (density ratio 3.9x matching 4-vs-1 sections, plus the serial loop in code) already constitute due diligence.

- MEASURE the success metric (Achieved compose concurrency >1 (measured via an event-loop heartbeat probe: max loop-gap during a section's phase-2 must be <2s where it previously froze for minutes) AND two sections' phase-2 progress interleaving AND the full-corpus render time trending down — WITHOUT any change to NLI/strict_verify faithfulness semantics (kept/dropped verdict set must stay identical to a serial control).) with a real number this round if at all possible.

Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). #!/bin/bash
# Record/replay A/B: certify kept/dropped verdict-set IDENTITY between a SERIAL control and the
# CONCURRENT (worker-thread) treatment created by the off-loop fix — at ZERO LLM cost on replay.
#   Phase 1 RECORD   : real LLM, 2 sections, capture judge+writer answers by content hash.
#   Phase 2 CONTROL  : replay, PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=1 + to_thread INLINED  (fully serial).
#   Phase 3 TREATMENT: replay, PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2 + real to_thread     (concurrent threads).
#   Phase 4 COMPARE  : assert byte-identical verified_text + verified/dropped counts per section.
# Runs MY worktree code only.
set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env 2>/dev/null; set +a
WT=/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix
cd "$WT" || exit 9
export PYTHONPATH="$WT" PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8

# activation flag slate (identical to the compose launcher)
export PG_COMPOSE_NO_RAW_SPAN_FALLBACK=1 PG_SECTION_BASKET_MAP=1 PG_SYNTH_PRIMARY=1
export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER=1 PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE=1 PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE_MULTICITED=1
export PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=enforce PG_WRITER_DEADLINE_TRANSPORT_AWARE=1
export PG_CROSS_SECTION_REPETITION_GUARD=1 PG_RENDER_SEAM_SANITIZE=1
export PG_WRITER_WALL_BASKET_SCALED=1 PG_WRITER_KSPAN_RECOVERY_PASS=1
export PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=16 PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES=1 PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY=8
# SMALL/FAST caps (do not affect the verdict mechanism; keep budget low)
export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=8 PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16
export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CALL_DEADLINE_S=120 PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_WALL_DEADLINE_S=400
export PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S=90 PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_MAX_TOKENS=8000
export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS=4000 PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS=8000 PG_S5_SPAN_CHAR_CAP=8000

CP2=/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s2_hamster_i1/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json
CP3=/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s3_gear/cp3_basket_snapshot.json
CP4=/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s4_gear/cp4_outline_snapshot.json
SECTIONS="${SECTIONS:-0,1}"
CAP="${CAP:-1}"
D="$WT/.replay_ab"
mkdir -p "$D"
STORE="$D/rr_store.pkl"

run() {  # $1=out $2=mode-desc ; env HB_* already set by caller
  python scripts/_hb_probe_run.py --cp2 "$CP2" --cp3 "$CP3" --cp4 "$CP4" \
    --out "$1" --sections "$SECTIONS" --cap-primary "$CAP"
}

echo "===================== PHASE 1: RECORD (real LLM) ====================="

export PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2

echo "===================== PHASE 2: CONTROL (serial replay) ====================="
export HB_REPLAY_MODE=replay HB_REPLAY_FILE="$STORE" HB_INLINE_TO_THREAD=1
export PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=1
run "$D/cp5_control.json" "control" || { echo "CONTROL FAILED"; exit 3; }

echo "===================== PHASE 3: TREATMENT (concurrent replay) ====================="
export HB_REPLAY_MODE=replay HB_REPLAY_FILE="$STORE" HB_INLINE_TO_THREAD=0
export PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2
run "$D/cp5_treatment.json" "treatment" || { echo "TREATMENT FAILED"; exit 4; }

echo "===================== PHASE 4: COMPARE ====================="
python scripts/_replay_compare.py "$D/cp5_control.json" "$D/cp5_treatment.json"
RC=$?
echo "=== replay A/B rc=$RC ==="
exit $RC

"""Compare two cp5 outputs (serial control vs concurrent treatment) for kept/dropped IDENTITY.

The kept sentence set of a section == its ``verified_text``; the dropped count == ``sentences_dropped``.
Byte-identical verified_text + identical verified/dropped counts across arms => the off-loop fix's
worker-thread concurrency changed NOTHING about the faithfulness verdict set. Exit 0 iff identical.
"""
import hashlib
import json
import sys


def _load(p):
    d = json.load(open(p, encoding="utf-8"))
    secs = {}
    for s in d["payload"]["section_drafts"]:
        secs[int(s["section_index"])] = {
            "verified_text": s.get("verified_text") or "",
            "sentences_verified": s.get("sentences_verified"),
            "sentences_dropped": s.get("sentences_dropped"),
            "is_gap_stub": s.get("is_gap_stub"),
        }
    return d, secs


def _sha(t):
    return hashlib.sha256(t.encode("utf-8", "surrogatepass")).hexdigest()[:16]


def main():
    ctrl_path, treat_path = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2]
    ctrl_d, ctrl = _load(ctrl_path)
    treat_d, treat = _load(treat_path)

    all_idx = sorted(set(ctrl) | set(treat))
    ok = True
    print(f"{'sec':>3} {'ctrl_v':>6} {'ctrl_d':>6} {'treat_v':>7} {'treat_d':>7} "
          f"{'ctrl_sha':>16} {'treat_sha':>16}  match")
    for i in all_idx:
        c = ctrl.get(i, {})
        t = treat.get(i, {})
        c_sha = _sha(c.get("verified_text", ""))
        t_sha = _sha(t.get("verified_text", ""))
        text_match = c.get("verified_text") == t.get("verified_text")
        cnt_match = (c.get("sentences_verified") == t.get("sentences_verified")
                     and c.get("sentences_dropped") == t.get("sentences_dropped"))
        m = text_match and cnt_match
        ok = ok and m
        print(f"{i:>3} {str(c.get('sentences_verified')):>6} {str(c.get('sentences_dropped')):>6} "
              f"{str(t.get('sentences_verified')):>7} {str(t.get('sentences_dropped')):>7} "
              f"{c_sha:>16} {t_sha:>16}  {'OK' if m else 'DIFFER'}")

    # whole-report identity too
    rep_c = ctrl_d["payload"]["assembled_report_md"]
    rep_t = treat_d["payload"]["assembled_report_md"]
    rep_match = rep_c == rep_t
    print(f"\nassembled_report_md: ctrl_sha={_sha(rep_c)} treat_sha={_sha(rep_t)} "
          f"len_ctrl={len(rep_c)} len_treat={len(rep_t)} match={rep_match}")
    ok = ok and rep_match

    print(f"\n=== VERDICT-SET IDENTITY: {'IDENTICAL (PASS)' if ok else 'DIVERGED (FAIL)'} ===")
